Title: CEP peptide and cytokinin pathways converge on CEPD glutaredoxins to inhibit root growth

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Coordinates plant responses to environmental and nutritional cues; inhibits primary root growth
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: glutaredoxin; class III glutaredoxin; mobile signaling regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Acts downstream of CEP and cytokinin signalling to mediate root growth inhibition; likely transcription-associated downstream effector rather than TF
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Convergence-point regulator required for full root growth inhibition by CEP and tZ
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes inhibition of primary root growth and meristem cell number in response to CEP and tZ
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double mutant analysis (cepd1,2); reciprocal hypocotyl grafting; segmented agar plate assays; qRT-PCR; GFP-CEPD1 fluorescence imaging
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CEPD1 transcript was induced by tZ in roots and by CEP3 in shoots. 2. GFP-CEPD1 fluorescence increased in root vasculature after tZ or CEP3 treatment and in cotyledon vasculature after CEP3 treatment. 3. Basal CEPD1 expression was reduced in ahk2,3. 4. cepd1,2 mutants were partially insensitive to both tZ and CEP3 for primary root growth inhibition. 5. Root CEPD activity mediated tZ response, whereas shoot CEPD activity mediated CEP response.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in roots by tZ; induced in roots and shoots by CEP3; GFP signal in primary root vasculature and cotyledon vasculature
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CEP3 peptide treatment; trans-zeatin treatment; nutrient-status context discussed (low/high nitrogen)
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: CEP peptide signalling via CEPR1; cytokinin signalling via AHK2/AHK3; trans-zeatin
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts at pathway convergence; CEP-CEPR1 signalling affects tZ levels and CEPD expression
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GFP-CEPD1 reporter line (promoter specifics not stated)
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted cytokinin metabolite profiling by UHPLC-MS/MS; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate integrator for engineering root growth responses to nutrient cues, but organ-specific action should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although historically described as downstream of CEP signalling for nitrate uptake, here CEPD1 is shown to function in both CEP and cytokinin root growth pathways with organ-specific circuitry

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Coordinates plant responses to environmental and nutritional cues; inhibits primary root growth
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: glutaredoxin; class III glutaredoxin; mobile signaling regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Acts downstream of CEP and cytokinin signalling to mediate root growth inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Together with CEPD1 required for full root growth inhibition by CEP and tZ
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to inhibition of primary root growth and meristem cell number in response to CEP and tZ
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double mutant analysis (cepd1,2); reciprocal hypocotyl grafting; segmented agar plate assays; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CEPD2 transcript in shoots was induced by CEP3. 2. CEPD2 transcript in roots was not significantly induced by tZ at 8 h. 3. cepd1,2 mutants were partially insensitive to both tZ and CEP3. 4. Together with CEPD1, CEPD2 contributes to organ-specific CEP and cytokinin response circuits.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in shoots in response to CEP3; root response to tZ not significant at tested time
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: CEP peptide signalling via CEPR1; cytokinin-related context
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted cytokinin metabolite profiling by UHPLC-MS/MS; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target with CEPD1 for tuning nutrient-responsive root growth
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study functionally tested CEPD1 and CEPD2 mainly as a double mutant, so gene-specific separation of effect is limited

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls adaptive growth responses to environmental and nutritional cues
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Perceives CEP peptides and modulates downstream CEPD signalling; affects cytokinin homeostasis and tZ sensitivity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required for full CEP response and contributes to tZ sensitivity/crosstalk
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CEPR1 reduces sensitivity to CEP3 and to tZ-mediated inhibition of primary root growth; alters tZ-type cytokinin levels in roots and shoots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutants (cepr1-3, cepr1-1); dose-response assays; cytokinin quantification by UHPLC-MS/MS
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cepr1 mutants were insensitive to CEP3 and showed ~10-fold reduced sensitivity to tZ. 2. CEP3 and tZ effects were additive in wild type but not in cepr1-3. 3. cepr1 mutants had perturbed cytokinin homeostasis, notably altered tZ-type cytokinin levels in roots and shoots.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Peptide-hormone signalling intersecting with cytokinin homeostasis and root growth control
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes CEPD1/2 expression in CEP signalling context; affects tZ-type cytokinin homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: CEP-CEPR1 signalling proposed to feed back on tZ levels in roots
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CEP3 overexpression line used, promoter not specified in provided text
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted cytokinin metabolite profiling by UHPLC-MS/MS; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to reprogram nutrient-responsive root architecture, though broad hormonal crosstalk may complicate deployment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary subject of crosstalk analysis; receptor mutant also altered cytokinin content, especially tZ-type cytokinins

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates cytokinin-dependent growth responses to environmental/nutritional cues
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Cytokinin perception; positively mediates CEP sensitivity and tZ induction of CEPD1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Together with AHK3, required for full CEP-mediated and cytokinin-mediated root growth inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AHK2 with AHK3 reduces CEP sensitivity; constitutive activation increases CEP sensitivity; ahk2,3 lowers basal CEPD1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double mutant analysis; gain-of-function mutants rock2/rock3; CEP3 dose-response; grafting; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ahk2,3 was partially insensitive to CEP3 and ~100-fold less sensitive in dose-response assays. 2. AHK2/3 function in both root and shoot contributed to CEP-mediated root inhibition. 3. Constitutively active AHK2 increased CEP sensitivity. 4. ahk2,3 had reduced basal CEPD1 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cytokinin perception in root growth inhibition and pathway crosstalk
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes basal CEPD1 expression and contributes to cytokinin-responsive CEPD1 induction
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted cytokinin metabolite profiling context; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to modulate nutrient-responsive root growth, but broad cytokinin developmental roles may impose pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes AHK2 together with AHK3 rather than alone
